subtasks.description:
Create src/tools/Inspector.mag — an object field viewer tool for the Alto TUI framework.

File header:
  namespace: 'Alto'
  import: 'Go'

Class declaration:
  Inspector subclass: View
    instanceVars: target fieldNames fieldValues fieldList valueView display parentWindow focusOnList

Note: focusOnList is a Boolean tracking which pane has focus (true = field list, false = value view).

## Class Method: open:on:

  classMethod: open: anObject on: aDisplay [
    | frame inspector fieldList valueView window |
    — Create frame: Rectangle x: 1 y: 1 width: 60 height: 20
    — Create inspector instance: Inspector new
    — Call inspector privateTarget: anObject
    — Build fieldNames and fieldValues from anObject:
      For Dictionary: fieldNames := anObject keys. fieldValues := array of (anObject at: key) printString for each key.
      Use Array new: 0 and copyWith: to build both arrays iteratively.
    — Create fieldList: ListView items: fieldNames onSelect: [:item | inspector onFieldSelected: item]
    — Create valueView: Label text: '' (or Label new with text: '')
    — Store fieldList and valueView on inspector via setter methods
    — Build Window: Window title: ('Inspector: ', anObject class name) content: inspector controller: inspector frame: frame
    — Store parentWindow and display on inspector
    — aDisplay addWindow: window
    — If fieldNames size > 0, set initial value view text to fieldValues at: 0
    — ^inspector
  ]

## classMethod: new
  Follow exact pattern from Workspace.mag:
    | instance |
    instance := super new.
    instance initialize.
    ^instance

## method: initialize
  super initialize.
  target := nil.
  fieldNames := Array new: 0.
  fieldValues := Array new: 0.
  fieldList := nil.
  valueView := nil.
  display := nil.
  parentWindow := nil.
  focusOnList := true

## Setter/getter methods (one-liner pattern matching Workspace.mag):
  privateTarget: anObject [ target := anObject ]
  privateFieldList: aList [ fieldList := aList ]
  privateValueView: aView [ valueView := aView ]
  parentWindow: aWindow [ parentWindow := aWindow ]
  display: aDisplay [ display := aDisplay ]
  target [ ^target ]
  fieldNames [ ^fieldNames ]
  fieldValues [ ^fieldValues ]
  fieldList [ ^fieldList ]
  valueView [ ^valueView ]
  parentWindow [ ^parentWindow ]
  focusOnList [ ^focusOnList ]

## method: onFieldSelected: item
  | idx |
  idx := fieldList selectedIndex.
  idx < fieldValues size ifTrue: [
    valueView text: (fieldValues at: idx)
  ].
  self invalidate

  Note: Also check if target is a Dictionary and the value at the selected key is itself a Dictionary — if so, on Enter, open a new Inspector on that value (drill-down). This is handled in handleEvent: below.

## method: handleEvent: evt
  | keyCode |
  (evt isKindOf: Go::V2::EventKey) ifFalse: [^self].
  keyCode := evt key.

  "Escape — close"
  keyCode = 27 ifTrue: [
    display notNil ifTrue: [display removeWindow: parentWindow].
    ^self
  ].

  "Tab — switch focus between field list and value view"
  keyCode = 9 ifTrue: [
    focusOnList := focusOnList not.
    self invalidate.
    ^self
  ].

  "Enter — drill-down if Dictionary value"
  (keyCode = 13 and: [focusOnList]) ifTrue: [
    | idx key val |
    — BUT all temp vars must be at method top! So declare idx, key, val at top of handleEvent:.
    idx := fieldList selectedIndex.
    (target isKindOf: Dictionary) ifTrue: [
      key := fieldNames at: idx.
      val := target at: key.
      (val isKindOf: Dictionary) ifTrue: [
        Inspector open: val on: display.
        ^self
      ]
    ].
    "For non-Dictionary values, just update the value view"
    self onFieldSelected: nil.
    ^self
  ].

  "Delegate arrow keys to fieldList when focused on list"
  focusOnList ifTrue: [
    fieldList handleEvent: evt.
    "Update value view after navigation"
    self onFieldSelected: nil.
    ^self
  ]

  IMPORTANT: ALL temp vars (keyCode, idx, key, val) must be declared at the TOP of the method:
  method: handleEvent: evt [
    | keyCode idx key val |
    ...
  ]

## method: displayOn: aCellBuffer
  Render the 2-pane layout:
  | listWidth valueX i lineStr style normalStyle reverseStyle fieldStr valStr |

  Left pane: field names list (roughly half width, e.g. listWidth := 20)
  Right pane: value text view (remaining width)

  normalStyle := Go::V2::Style new.
  reverseStyle := (Go::V2::Style new) reverse: true.

  — Draw separator line (vertical bar char 9474) at x = listWidth for each row
  — Left pane: render each visible fieldName. If it's the selected index, use reverseStyle. Use aCellBuffer putString:x:y:style: or putChar:x:y:style: character by character.
  — Right pane: render the currently selected value's text starting at x = listWidth + 1. Word-wrap or truncate to fit.

  Implementation approach for left pane:
    visibleCount := aCellBuffer height.
    i := 0.
    [i < visibleCount] whileTrue: [
      itemIndex := fieldList scrollOffset + i.   "Note: scrollOffset is on ListView but may not be accessible — if not, track scroll state here or just use i directly"
      (itemIndex < fieldNames size) ifTrue: [
        fieldStr := fieldNames at: itemIndex.
        "Truncate to listWidth - 1 chars"
        fieldStr size > (listWidth - 1) ifTrue: [fieldStr := fieldStr copyFrom: 0 to: listWidth - 1].
        (itemIndex = fieldList selectedIndex)
          ifTrue: [style := reverseStyle]
          ifFalse: [style := normalStyle].
        aCellBuffer putString: fieldStr x: 0 y: i style: style
      ].
      "Draw separator"
      aCellBuffer putChar: 9474 x: listWidth y: i style: normalStyle.
      i := i + 1
    ].

  Right pane:
    idx := fieldList selectedIndex.
    idx < fieldValues size ifTrue: [
      valStr := fieldValues at: idx.
      "Render value text in right pane — simple single-line for MVP"
      aCellBuffer putString: valStr x: listWidth + 2 y: 0 style: normalStyle
    ].

  ALL temp vars at method top:
    | listWidth visibleCount i itemIndex fieldStr valStr idx style normalStyle reverseStyle |

## KEY CONVENTIONS (MUST follow):
- namespace: 'Alto', import: 'Go'
- ALL temp vars declared at method top, never mid-block
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — use isNil ifTrue: or notNil ifTrue:
- No mid-block | var | declarations
- Array growth: Array new: 0 then copyWith:
- Styles: Go::V2::Style new and (Go::V2::Style new) reverse: true — NOT Dictionaries
- Integer char codes (9474 for vertical bar, 32 for space, etc.)
- Subclass View (like Workspace and Transcript)
- Use the exact constructor pattern from Workspace.mag (classMethod: new, classMethod: open:on:)
- Event keys: 27=Escape, 13=Enter, 9=Tab, 258=Up, 259=Down, 260=Left, 261=Right, 256=KeyRune
- Events: (evt isKindOf: Go::V2::EventKey) ifFalse: [^self] guard at top of handleEvent:
- String indexing is 0-based: (str at: i) value for char code
- Array at: uses 0-based indexing in this codebase (see fieldValues at: idx)

## KNOWN CAVEATS from review history:
- ListView has a known off-by-one (items at: selectedIndex + 1) that was accepted as-is. The Inspector should NOT use ListView's selectedItem method directly for indexing into its own fieldValues array. Instead use fieldList selectedIndex to get the 0-based index and index into fieldValues with that directly.
- ListView uses Dictionary styles internally for selection highlighting — this is a pre-existing issue in ListView.mag. The Inspector's own rendering should use Go::V2::Style objects.
- ListView handleEvent: expects Dictionary events with #key/#rune keys, not Go::V2::EventKey objects. The Inspector must either: (a) convert Go::V2::EventKey to Dictionary format before delegating to fieldList, OR (b) re-implement arrow navigation directly in Inspector's handleEvent: without delegating to ListView. Option (b) is safer — handle Up/Down in Inspector by directly manipulating fieldList's selectedIndex.

Since ListView.handleEvent: expects Dictionary events (evt at: #key), but our Inspector gets Go::V2::EventKey objects, the safest approach is to handle arrow navigation directly:

  keyCode = 258 ifTrue: [  "Up"
    ... decrement selection, clamp, update value view, invalidate ...
    ^self
  ].
  keyCode = 259 ifTrue: [  "Down"
    ... increment selection, clamp, update value view, invalidate ...
    ^self
  ].

But this means we need access to ListView's selectedIndex setter. If ListView doesn't expose a selectedIndex: setter, manage selection state in Inspector directly with a local selectedIndex ivar, and just pass items to ListView for display. OR add selectedIndex: to ListView (but that's outside scope). Best approach: manage selection in Inspector's own ivar and rebuild ListView items display accordingly, OR just implement the full navigation in Inspector without delegating.

RECOMMENDED SIMPLEST APPROACH: Don't use ListView widget at all for the left pane. Instead, render the field list manually in displayOn: (like Workspace renders text lines), and track selectedIndex as an Inspector instance var. This avoids all ListView API compatibility issues. The Inspector already has fieldNames array — just render them with selection highlighting directly. This is the approach Workspace takes (it doesn't use a sub-widget for text display).

If this approach is taken, remove fieldList from instanceVars and add selectedIndex and scrollOffset instead:
  instanceVars: target fieldNames fieldValues selectedIndex scrollOffset valueView display parentWindow focusOnList

This simplifies the implementation significantly and avoids all ListView compatibility issues.

Create test/test_inspector.mag — comprehensive tests for the Inspector tool.

Follow the exact test runner pattern from test/test_phase5.mag:
- Test runner class TI (or T6) subclass: Object with passed/failed/failures ivars
- assert:message: and assertEqual:to:message: methods
- report method
- EvtHelper for creating test events

File header:
  namespace: 'Alto'
  import: 'Go'

Tests to write (use test IDs like INS1, INS2, etc.):

## Construction Tests
- INS1: Inspector responds to open:on: — verify class method exists
- INS2: Inspector open: (Dictionary new) on: nil — creates Inspector instance (non-nil)
- INS3: Inspector has target set to the Dictionary passed in
- INS4: Inspector has fieldNames matching Dictionary keys
- INS5: Inspector has fieldValues matching Dictionary values as strings
- INS6: Inspector parentWindow is a Window
- INS7: parentWindow title starts with 'Inspector:'
- INS8: Inspector subclasses View

## Dictionary Inspection Tests
- INS9: Empty Dictionary — fieldNames size = 0, fieldValues size = 0
- INS10: Dictionary with 3 keys — fieldNames size = 3
- INS11: Values are formatted as strings (printString)

## Navigation Tests (using Dictionary events with #key)
- INS12: Initial selectedIndex is 0
- INS13: Down arrow increments selectedIndex
- INS14: Up arrow decrements selectedIndex
- INS15: Up arrow at 0 stays at 0 (clamp)
- INS16: Down arrow at last item stays at last (clamp)

## Tab Focus Tests
- INS17: Initial focus is on field list (focusOnList = true)
- INS18: Tab switches focusOnList to false
- INS19: Tab again switches back to true

## Escape Tests
- INS20: Escape with nil display does not crash

## Enter/Drill-down Tests
- INS21: Enter on a field whose value is a Dictionary — verify it doesn't crash (drill-down creates new Inspector, but without a real display this just tests it doesn't error)
- INS22: Enter on a field whose value is a String — does not open new Inspector

## Value Display Tests
- INS23: After construction with non-empty Dictionary, value view text should show first field's value
- INS24: After navigating down, value view should update to show new field's value

## Convention Compliance Tests
- INS25: File starts with namespace: 'Alto'
- INS26: File has import: 'Go'

Create test events as Dictionaries (matching the pattern from test_phase5.mag):
  For Go::V2::EventKey simulation, check how Inspector handles events. If Inspector uses (evt isKindOf: Go::V2::EventKey), tests need to either:
  (a) Create actual Go::V2::EventKey objects if possible, or
  (b) Test internal methods like onFieldSelected: and navigation state directly by calling setter methods.

RECOMMENDED: Since we can't easily create Go::V2::EventKey objects in tests, test the Inspector by:
1. Constructing it with open:on: (passing nil for display to avoid real terminal)
2. Directly manipulating state and calling methods
3. If Inspector re-implements navigation without delegating to ListView, we can test navigation by calling handleEvent: with Dictionary events — but ONLY if Inspector's handleEvent: doesn't guard with isKindOf: Go::V2::EventKey. If it does guard, test navigation methods directly.

ALL temp vars at method top. Integer char codes. No ifNil:/ifNotNil:. Array new: 0 + copyWith:.

Review src/tools/Inspector.mag and test/test_inspector.mag for correctness and convention compliance.

Review checklist:

## Critical Convention Checks
1. File starts with namespace: 'Alto' and import: 'Go'
2. Class uses instanceVars: keyword form (not bracket syntax)
3. ALL temp vars declared at method top — no mid-block | var | declarations anywhere
4. No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — only isNil ifTrue: or notNil ifTrue:
5. Array growth uses Array new: 0 and copyWith: — never add: or remove:
6. Styles are Go::V2::Style objects — NOT Dictionaries (no Dictionary at: #attrs put: #reverse)
7. Integer char codes (9474 for vertical bar, 32 for space, 27 for escape, etc.) — no dollar-sign char literals
8. Subclasses View (like Workspace and Transcript)

## API Correctness Checks
9. Constructor follows Workspace pattern: classMethod: new calls super new + initialize
10. open:on: creates Window with proper title, frame (60x20), adds to display
11. Dictionary inspection: keys become fieldNames, values (as strings) become fieldValues
12. Event handling guards with (evt isKindOf: Go::V2::EventKey) ifFalse: [^self] — OR handles Dictionary events consistently
13. Escape (27) removes window from display
14. Tab (9) toggles focus between panes
15. Enter (13) on Dictionary value opens new Inspector (drill-down)
16. Arrow keys (258/259) navigate field selection
17. displayOn: renders 2-pane layout with separator

## Known Pitfalls to Check
18. Does NOT use ListView's selectedItem method for indexing (known off-by-one bug)
19. If delegating to ListView.handleEvent:, events must be Dictionary format (not Go::V2::EventKey) — OR navigation is handled directly without delegation
20. String indexing is 0-based: (str at: i) value
21. Array indexing: verify consistency with rest of codebase
22. No calls to nonexistent methods (add:, remove:, removeAt:, indexOf:)

## Test File Checks
23. Tests use integer char codes, not dollar-sign literals
24. Test runner pattern matches codebase convention
25. Tests cover: construction, Dictionary inspection, navigation, tab focus, escape, enter/drill-down, value display
26. Tests don't depend on real Display (use nil)

Report verdict as: pass, fail (with specific bugs listed), or observation (for non-blocking issues).
